og.title: Water Damage Litigation Support — Free CARSI Course
og.description: A free CARSI course on forensic water-damage investigation — moisture source tracing, psychrometric evidence, scope analysis, report writing and preparing for expert testimony.

faq.q: What does water damage litigation support involve?
faq.a: It is the forensic side of restoration: tracing moisture sources, analysing material failure, assessing code compliance, gathering psychrometric and sensor data, benchmarking scope and cost, and preparing evidence and reports that stand up in legal proceedings.

faq.q: Do I need prior experience to take this course?
faq.a: No. It is an introduction that aims to give a basic but comprehensive understanding of forensic investigations, so it suits restoration professionals who are new to litigation-support work. There are no prerequisites.

faq.q: What evidence and documentation does the course cover?
faq.a: It covers psychrometric records, sensor-mapping history, intrusive and laboratory sampling, demonstrative diagrams, catalogued technical literature, financial audit trails, evidence chain of custody, and expert-testimony scripting.

faq.q: How much does the course cost?
faq.a: It is free. The course is published at no cost and can be taken online in Australian English.

courseJsonLd.name: Introduction to Water Damage Litigation Support

courseJsonLd.coursePrerequisites: None — introductory course for restoration professionals.
